runs.llm_decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.3°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The most effective primary lever is to increase the heat spreader conductivity to improve lateral heat distribution away from the chip hotspot. I will apply a conservative 1.5x increase (from 90.0 to 135.0 W/(m*K)), which is within the allowed [0.5x, 2.0x] step ratio and well below the upper bound of 500.0.

runs.llm_decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). Since the spreader conductivity is already at its maximum bound (500.0), further material improvement is impossible. Following the optimization history suggestion to try a different variable category, I will increase the heat spreader width to enhance lateral heat spreading area, which is the next most effective lever for reducing peak temperature.
